sector.name: EMS
sector.description: EMS players moving deeper into components

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹12,186 Cr (+22% YoY), while operating EBITDA stood at ₹970 Cr (+22% YoY)
Adjusted PAT stood at ₹338 Cr (+22% YoY)
Consumer Durable revenue stood at ₹8,383 Cr (+14% YoY), despite a largely flat RAC industry
Electronics revenue stood at ₹3,268 Cr (+49% YoY), while operating EBITDA grew 89%
Railway Systems & Defence revenue stood at ₹535 Cr (+19% YoY)
highlights: FY26 was strong despite a challenging RAC industry
Electronics was the key outperformer, supported by PCBA, bare PCB and acquisitions
Power-One, Unitronics and Shogini were acquired during FY26
Amber received more than ₹4,500 Cr investment approvals under ECMS for PCB expansion
Ascent-K HDI PCB facility trial production is expected in Q3FY28
Net debt reduced to ₹511 Cr vs ₹780 Cr last year
Amber Enterprises India Ltd.quote: "Amber is outperforming the sector on diversification, PCB expansion, Electronics growth, Railways & Defence order visibility and balance sheet improvement."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹48,893 Cr (+26% YoY)
EBITDA excluding exceptional gain stood at ₹1,887 Cr (+23% YoY)
PAT after minority interest excluding exceptional gain stood at ₹845 Cr (+20% YoY)
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹10,520 Cr, while EBITDA excluding exceptional gain stood at ₹418 Cr
FY26 smartphone exports stood around 4-4.5 Mn units
highlights: Q4 revenue was flat due to geopolitics, soft demand, inventory rationalisation and input cost pressure
Smartphone and IT hardware segments were most impacted in Q4
Management is accelerating backward integration and localisation
Q Tech camera module capacity is being expanded to around 180-190 Mn units
Display backward integration is expected to play out more meaningfully in FY27-FY28
Company is exploring server manufacturing and industrial EMS as future adjacencies
Dixon Technologies India Ltd.quote: "Dixon is outperforming the sector on scale, customer depth, mobile EMS leadership, backward integration and multiple growth engines."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹3,626 Cr (+33% YoY), while EBITDA stood at ₹574 Cr (+40% YoY)
FY26 EBITDA margin stood at 16%, while PAT stood at ₹364 Cr
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹1,243 Cr (+26% YoY), while EBITDA stood at ₹194 Cr
Q4FY26 EBITDA margin stood at 16%, while PAT stood at ₹91 Cr
Core EMS working capital days reduced from 83 days in FY24 to 53 days in FY26

guidance.value: Outgrow EMS market and double market growth rate
Target near 30% of revenue over coming years

highlights: FY26 was a consolidation year focused on execution capability
Topline was below expectations due to disruptions, deferments and supply-chain delays
Order book remains healthy, diversified and non-cancellable
Core EMS growth is broad-based across automotive, EV, industrial, aerospace and railways
OSAT Unit 1 is fully operational and Unit 2 is moving towards commercialisation
Company is moving from traditional EMS to ESDM and product-driven enterprise
Kaynes Technology India Ltd.quote: "Kaynes is outperforming the sector on margin profile, OSAT optionality, PCB pipeline, product-led transition and high-value EMS positioning."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹4,857 Cr (+27% YoY), while operating EBITDA stood at ₹545 Cr (+68% YoY)
Operating EBITDA margin stood at 11%, while reported EBITDA margin stood at 12%
PAT stood at ₹346 Cr (+87% YoY), with PAT margin of 7%
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹1,477 Cr (+56% YoY and +16% QoQ)
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹119 Cr (+67% YoY), with PAT margin of 8%

guidance.value: Confident of sustaining or exceeding FY26 revenue, EBITDA and OCF

highlights: FY26 was a landmark year with strong execution, margin expansion and balance sheet improvement
Company exceeded initial guidance on EBITDA, revenue growth, exports and operating cash flow
Exports crossed ₹1,200 Cr and contributed 24-25% of operating revenue
ODM revenue increased to ₹825 Cr and contributed 17% of revenue
Automotive grew 39%, industrial grew 30% and healthcare grew 36%
Working capital cycle improved from 69 days to 63 days
Syrma SGS Technology Ltd.quote: "Syrma is outperforming the sector on PAT growth, margin expansion, exports, ODM mix, working capital discipline and diversified vertical mix."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹1,603 Cr (+46% YoY), with gross margin of 34%
FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹173 Cr (+51% YoY), while PAT stood at ₹113 Cr (+78% YoY)
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹480 Cr (+40% YoY and +15% QoQ)
Q4FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹57 Cr (+38% YoY), while PAT stood at ₹41 Cr (+70% YoY)
Net working capital stood at 112 days vs guided range of 120-130 days

guidance.value: Expected in later part of FY27

highlights: FY26 was Avalon's best year and Q4 was the 7th consecutive quarter of growth
India manufacturing contributed 77% of Q4 revenue with 16.7% EBITDA margin
Industrial contributed 34% of revenue and grew 65%
Box-build share increased to 56% in Q4FY26
Kavach anti-collision system has completed testing and is on track for commercial production
Semiconductor equipment volume production is expected in FY27
Avalon Technologies Ltd.quote: "Avalon is outperforming the sector on order book visibility, working capital improvement, ROCE, mission-critical box-builds and global manufacturing flexibility."

fyPerformance: FY26 consolidated revenue stood at ₹5,288 Cr vs ₹4,869 Cr last year
FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹442 Cr vs ₹519 Cr last year
FY26 PAT stood at ₹194 Cr vs ₹291 Cr last year
RAC revenue grew 9% in FY26 but declined 12% in Q4FY26
Washing machine business grew 52% in FY26 and around 70% in Q4FY26

guidance.value: 2 Mn units Phase 1, expandable to 4 Mn

highlights: FY26 was difficult for RAC-led consumer durable manufacturing
RAC industry declined around 15% during FY26
Q4FY26 was impacted by truck shortage and deferment of dispatches into April
Management estimated Q4 revenue loss of around ₹420 Cr from disruptions
Commodity inflation and currency depreciation led to around 250 bps gross margin impact in Q4
Moulding facility consolidation at Salarpur should improve cost structure over FY27-FY28
PG Electroplast Ltd.quote: "PG is not outperforming the sector on FY26 profitability, but it remains a strong recovery candidate if RAC demand normalises and backward integration ramps up."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹1,894 Cr, down 13% YoY
FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹114 Cr, down 28% YoY, with EBITDA margin of 6%
Net profit stood at ₹3 Cr
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹591 Cr, down 8% YoY
Q4FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹26 Cr, down 64% YoY

guidance.value: Better trends as customer conditions and inventory normalize

highlights: FY26 was challenging due to temporary RAC industry headwinds
EPACK added 5 new customers in Q4FY26 and 17 customers in FY26
Total active customer base reached 72
SDA and LDA segment grew around 53% YoY in Q4FY26
Component business grew around 50% YoY in Q4FY26
Hisense partnership became operational in Q4FY26
Epack Durable Ltd.quote: "EPACK is building strong triggers through customer additions, non-RAC growth, components, Hisense ramp-up and Bhiwadi utilisation."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹823 Cr (+18% YoY), while EBITDA stood at ₹86 Cr
FY26 EBITDA margin stood at 10%, while PAT stood at ₹15 Cr
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹317 Cr vs ₹240 Cr last year
Revenue CAGR since FY21 stood at 49%
EBITDA CAGR since FY21 stood at 47% and PAT CAGR stood at 52%

guidance.value: 1 Mn to 1.8 Mn units before next season
2.8 Mn to 6 Mn units by March

highlights: FY26 was a year of resilience after a difficult H1
Strong H2 and Q4 helped the company recover for the full year
Company has built capacity across EMS & lighting, AC, refrigeration and compressors
AC dependence reduced from 70-75% earlier to around 60%
New tool room in Nashik is operational and should support faster product development
EMS new facility is expected to come online in around 3 months
Virtuoso Optoelectronics Ltd.quote: "Virtuoso is outperforming smaller peers on diversification, capacity creation, compressor optionality and H2 recovery, but PAT margin remains a key monitorable."
